expectations.text: References or applies the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle (or test-driven development) for skill authoring
expectations.evidence: Opening line: 'Creating a skill follows a strict TDD cycle: RED (baseline test) -> GREEN (write minimal skill) -> REFACTOR (close loopholes).'

expectations.text: Mentions pressure scenarios, failure modes, or edge cases as the starting point for skill design
expectations.evidence: Step 2 says 'Design 3+ pressure scenarios' and lists three specific ones: time pressure + exhaustion, interpersonal pressure, low-signal pressure.

expectations.text: Advises starting by identifying what goes wrong WITHOUT the skill (watching the agent fail), before writing instructions
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'Before writing a single line of the skill, you need to see what an agent does without it. This is the Iron Law: no skill without a failing test first.'

expectations.text: Includes a testing/verification step where the skill is tested against pressure scenarios after drafting
expectations.evidence: Step 3 subsection: 'Run the same pressure scenarios WITH the skill loaded' followed by four expected compliance behaviors.
